title: Physaliastrum heterophyllum
intro: Physaliastrum heterophyllum, also known as the Korean angelica, is a perennial plant species native to East Asia, characterized by its distinctive umbrella-shaped inflorescence and edible rhizome. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Physaliastrum heterophyllum include lignans, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Physaliastrum heterophyllum is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture to treat various health conditions, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.

Its roots and leaves are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to reduce inflammation.
